title: Relative Flux And Luminosity Proxies
summary: Convert ugriz magnitudes into linear relative-flux, flux-share, spectral-moment, and redshift-scaled luminosity-proxy features to test whether a physical photometric representation adds signal beyond magnitude-space colors.
feature_strategy: Keep the raw numeric and categorical columns, but add a parallel representation where each magnitude band is converted to relative flux as 10^(-0.4*m) after clipping only invalid/sentinel values. Derive total_flux, log_total_flux, per-band flux fractions, adjacent and broad flux ratios, normalized SED moments over approximate SDSS filter wavelengths, flux-weighted mean wavelength, flux concentration/entropy, blue-to-red flux balance, and redshift-scaled luminosity proxies such as log_flux_band + 2*log1p(redshift) and total_flux * (1+redshift)^2. Include simple interactions with spectral_type and galaxy_population only as one-hot or native categorical inputs; do not add target encodings, auxiliary labels, nearest-neighbor density, sky grids, or heavy ensemble logic in this initial test.
baseline_model_panel: Use one simple balanced linear model on standardized numeric plus one-hot categorical features, one shallow/randomized tree ensemble with balanced class weights, and one class-weighted gradient-boosted tree/categorical-boosting model such as LightGBM, XGBoost, or CatBoost with balanced multiclass weighting.
model_panel_rationale: The linear model tests whether the flux representation creates separable additive structure, while shallow/randomized trees and a class-weighted boosted tree test nonlinear thresholds and interactions without making model tuning the experimental variable.
validation_strategy: Use 5-fold StratifiedKFold with shuffled folds and balanced accuracy; compute all transformations from covariates only, fitting scalers or imputers inside each fold where applicable.
materialization_hint: Build a feature function that starts from clean numeric magnitudes and redshift, creates flux columns with safe clipping to avoid overflow, computes normalized flux features row-wise, appends raw categories, then evaluates the same feature matrix across the baseline panel with per-fold OOF scores and per-class recall diagnostics.
expected_signal: If useful, CV should improve most for STAR/QSO separation or reduce confusion in faint/high-redshift objects, with tree models benefiting from flux fractions and linear models improving from log-flux and normalized SED moments; feature importance should surface flux_share_u/g/z, flux entropy, and redshift-scaled luminosity proxies.
risk: This overlaps partially with prior magnitude color and SED-shape hypotheses because magnitude differences are mathematically related to flux ratios, so gains may be small; luminosity proxies are approximate without a real cosmology and could add noisy transformations if synthetic data generation already encoded the signal in raw magnitudes.

autogluon_feature_transfer.source: legacy feature engineering only; model, fold, calibration, and blending logic intentionally omitted
